item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So does this SCP come from an alternate reality where the Swedish Sky Moose exists and Florida is its own country? Or are those delusions which are side effects of manufacturing and drinking anomalous soft drinks?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think that's the point, is that he's playing up the "American is stupid and can't even remember his own country's name" schtick for laughs, hence,
countries like Florida, all-Armenian, and the other numerous references to other places with names that sound like America.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There are a lot of SCPs that haven't aged well, but I think this one is an example of an SCP that has aged exceptionally well. The social commentary underlying this is even more relevant in 2018 than it was when this was written.

item.title: Image Change and Attribution
item.content:encoded:
Hijacking Voct's comment so it's on the first page of the discussion; I legitimately cant' find the original image I used for this article, or anything else about it, other than it may be watermelon soda; however, as someone on the reading of the skip on Youtube pointed out, it may very well be Green River soda, and it's a dead ringer for it.

Image taken from Wikimedia under a CC License:
https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Green_River_soft_drink.jpg

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've always been a fan of root beer, but IDK about that creamy sarsaparilla.
+1 because I thought it was fun.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Roddy Root really got violent quickly. He gives me the creeps, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I am pretty upset, because I looked at the youtube comments of someone reading the video and everyone is choosing the root bear and not Lime Liftoff! I love fruity drinks, but it seems like a lot more people like root beer… :/

"All-Armenian"

Misspell? I won't be editing willy nilly like last time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So we have the "LET THE BURN" root beer folk vs the elitist limers. What's that, the limes are incomprehensible overlords?
+1

item.title: Down with the Limies Lies!
item.content:encoded:
Down-voted for now. A lot of the science in regard to the effects of the two drinks seems too far-fetched. It may work better to note observations of nondescript mutagenic effects on the drinkers, then allow the reader to figure them out from the adverts.

This would shorten the article and lead the reader to point where the article is strongest. Those adverts are where the creepy lies, not the pseudo-science that makes little sense.

I love the concept. It's scary. But I think it can be better. I will rerate if any change is made.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I used to adore Fruit Twist, but, like all soft fizzy drinks, it's too sweet for me now. Makes me feel a bit ill thinking about it.

… unlike this article, which I read again, and still love.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fanta makes some pretty good soda flavors. Peach Fanta is my favorite. *-*

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Coming back to this, I'm fairly sure that picture is of Fanta Melon Soda. It's pretty good, like this article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You had me at the "low levels of sodium benzoate" (and indeed you made me go and look that up). The inability of the companies to figure out exactly where they are was magnificent, too, and I loved the Fallout vibe. Hilarious and vaguely threatening.

item.title: U- UP- UPVOTE!!!
item.content:encoded: You can have my upvotez. 1+

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A Møøse once bit my sister…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"the remarkably low level of sodium benzoate"

And I upvoted.

This thing was great, the only troubles are the science behind it being a bit… unsound, but that's not enough to prevent me from upvoting it. You still might want to do something about that though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: IIRC there never was a media war SCP, so that's a good thing. Decent execution, if a tad bit long. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's definitely not flawless. The science should either be better or more obfuscated, the containment needs work, and the fact that it was even containable strains credulity.

Having said that, it's awesome, I enjoyed reading it and I'd be happy to see more articles like it on the site, so +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My first thought after seeing the page image being a glass of green liquid was "Thank god I only took a little sip!" So I think it's very important to establish that you're okay if you only have a little sip.

+1'd.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What gimmick?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Downvoted for science fail after we talked about this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I want to see a tale/article about the Swedish Sky Moose.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: … It's like if Coke and Pepsi took an entire town and turned them into the Hatfields and McCoys. My hat to you, sir, and of course the only +1 it is in my power to give.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you for acknowledging Florida's sovereignty, but I have to neutral vote this on account of how gimmicky it is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Obligatory "Neither does your mom!"

Your mom jokes. The universal comeback.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sorry Scan, that makes no sense.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

I love the silly Fallout/Bioshock-esque class warfare going on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think I like that, actually. I also like the "Countries like… Florida."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, well, so does your mom.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I realized that within 10 seconds of posting, sadly Powerpoint has limitations making it much harder to add detail, but I'm gonna try

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
it's no match for my
WATERMELON WATERBOARD!!!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The only thing that makes me sad about this is that Sasparilla Cream comes from Carl's Caffeinated Club but is not actually caffeinated.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I 2nd this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: NO! I will not allow the glorious Swedish Sky Mööse to suffer like this any longer! They have ruled the glorious Swedish Empire for centuries, and I will not see it be taken from them! I will gather an army of mööse from all over the world, and together we will free the mööse from the tyranny and oppression of Citrox and all others who would seek to enslave them! All the Sky Mööse! All hail the Mööse! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: it looks like a dildo :p

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: POMEGRANATE POWER PUNCH for the win!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: MANDARIN MUTILATION

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Me: "Ho hum. This is fun."

Article: "Let them burn."

Me: "…. :D " +1

I like the addition of the class-war aspect, too. It's a bit OTT, sure, but it's nicely done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Never underestimate the possibilities of Powerpoint(:

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
MORE CARL'S CAFFEINE CLUB!
BIRCH BEER BLAST!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh my god dude.

Oh my fucking god. This is incredible.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Why did I decide to create this?
Because I felt like it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Now that you mention it, you're exactly right.

This combines several different elements in an excellent way. Good work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have no words… seriously, I can't think of anything to say right now!
I had no idea that you were considering that exact same name. I got 2 other names for Citrox Products, but for the sake of preventing spoilers, I might PM them to you

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …are you fucking psychic? I swear to god, I was going to include an addendum where that was a product that was going to be introduced.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Am I the only one who prefers Citrox's lesser known product: Orange Oblivion™?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There are a few lazy redactions, and this:

"or those of you who have had enough Lime Liftoff to gain the Special Liftoff Power® and pass out fliers and free samples to your friends! "

is phrased in a way that doesn't really make sense, but other than that I like it. Upvoted, because neither of those are enough to bother me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Incorrect use of "termination" in the ad with the guns. "Terminate" is a specific euphemism for when the Foundation intentionally executes someone.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Citrox Corporation waives all responsibility for fatalities caused by drinking Lime Liftoff

Would change to

Citrox Corporation does not claim responsibility for any injuries or deaths related to drinking Lime Liftoff

Also: Florida's a country???

item.title: Re: Cola Wars!
item.content:encoded:
… What?
E: My bad. I thought that you were talking about the previous post. :P
I think that was intentional.

item.title: Re: Cola Wars!
item.content:encoded: Wait a sec…Florida's a country???/

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh PUL-EEEZ.
I won a Carl's Caffeine Club Incindiary Grenade(tm) in the super sasparilla contest.
Being filled with hydrogen doesn't sound so good now, does it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Good lord.

Not only does this just get wackier and wackier as you read, it's awfully clever as social commentary. I think my favorite part is that neither corporation seems to know precisely where it is they are.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not breathing won't help you to avoid get burnt by my citrox flamethower™ (only for 60 tickets, can you imagine that?).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No way! Sasparilla FTW.
Who needs to float when you don't even need to breath?

item.title: Re: Cola Wars!
item.content:encoded:
As a Floridian, seconded. We might as well be a separate banana republic with some of the stuff that happens(and, coincidentally, might have a little bit of truth in there since Florida is reported to have a lot of trafficking compared to most states).

I'm especially liking how each side is intentionally trying to attract demographics that can be pitted against each other. While there's so much emphasis of them being enemies, I get the idea in the back of my head that both sides manufacture the war to mutually benefit each other.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think commercial skips are becoming popular these days. This one was interesting in the way 2 corporations would try to destroy eachother. Oh well, sarsaparrilla was a bit OTT compared to citrox. +1

P.S: I'd drink citrox and smash sarsaparrilla bottles for those tickets :D

item.title: Cola Wars!
item.content:encoded:
You had me at:

Slaves in countries like Columbia, Chile and Florida…

+1

Reminds me of the Rock-And-Roller Cola Wars, but taken OTT (for those who remember the 80's).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hm. There's a fair bit I like about this, coupled with stuff I really don't care for. I feel the ads are kind of silly, but not in an out of control way. That said, I'm not sure it's a good silliness. I like the Citrox ones, but Caffeine Club ones read somewhat off. I'd recommend looking at SCP-186 as an SCP similar to what you're going for.

I also feel there is some tacked on stuff. Most notably the reacting to what people say bit. This is underdeveloped, unconnected to the other anomalies, and doesn't really add much to the article. It would be cleaner without it. The containment procedures are also off. In addition to the same problems as with the first incarnation of the Rumor Mill, it's unclear why the Foundation is containing this onsite. Wouldn't it make more sense to round up all the affected individuals and just contain them (separated, of course) at some Foundation site? Also, 5km perimeter is ambiguous. Do you mean a perimeter with length of 5km, or a perimeter in a circle with radius 5km? Each has the Foundation stationing a gargantuan number of armed personnel. 250 if it's 5km in length, and a whopping 1571 if it's a circle.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I thought you were going to include a threat level in addition to object class. You could start a trend.
Nonetheless, have an upvote.
